name: Benzoate sensor (BenM + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects benzoate, a common aromatic pollutant and degradation intermediate. The BenM LysR-family regulator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a visible pigment, in P. putida KT2440.

input.operatingRange: BenM responds across µM benzoate / cis,cis-muconate

sensing.sensorMechanism: BenM (LysR family) activates the benABC promoter in response to benzoate and its metabolite cis,cis-muconate.
sensing.signalFlow: Benzoate -> BenM activates PbenA -> transcribes an anti-pigment sgRNA -> CRISPRi represses a constitutive amilCP cassette -> pigment fades with benzoate (NOT). Pair an inverter for colour-on.

performance.notes: BenM is a well-studied aromatic sensor; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: P. putida KT2440 certified HV1 safety host; add containment for field use.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-pigment sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the amilCP promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: BenM + PbenA -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + constitutive amilCP. SEVA vectors.

buildSteps.title: Transform P. putida KT2440
buildSteps.description: Electroporate; select; confirm baseline pigment without benzoate.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add soil eluate/water + benzoate standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the BenM benzoate-sensing module with P. putida CRISPRi
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
